notes: Riplash is your hook — landing it is the whole game; pull a squishy into your team, then Shock Therapy to break their shield and silence.
Skewer suppresses everyone in front of you — angle it to catch multiple enemies, ideally after Riplash drags one in.
Riot Shield builds when you damage heroes with Riplash/Electrocute — stay aggressive to keep the self-shield up and tank for your team.
Sticky Mine has a 2.5s fuse — stick it before the fight or combo it with CC so the target can't simply walk out of the blast.
Tesla Dome zones an entire area — drop it on an objective or chokepoint to deny space, not just for the damage.
Farm Field Data stacks relentlessly — your magic power grows permanently from kills and damage, so prioritize CS and poke early.
Behemoth gives a huge health pool and empowers your abilities — ult before engaging, not as a panic button, so you fight the whole duration as a giant.
Boulder Throw can be held — carry it to body-block skillshots, then throw for damage when the enemy commits.
King of the Jungle's regen makes you relentless — retreat to heal and re-engage rather than dying for a single fight.
Mortal Coil's orb pulls enemies in — combo it with Stupor or Soulfire so they can't walk out of your follow-up.
Abyssal Rift teleports you in — use it onto a backline, but only commit with an escape or peel ready, since you're squishy.
Pick up Essence (Echoes of the Damned) from kills — fight near dying minions and heroes to keep yourself topped up.
Fly Trap pulls grouped enemies together — land it on a clump, then drop Untamed Growth and Bramble Patch on top for massive AoE DoT.
Stack Harvest Nettles on one target — repeated hits ramp the damage, so keep hitting the same enemy rather than spreading it.
You can't escape (Mobility 1, Durability 1) — zone with Bramble Patch to keep divers off you and stay deep behind your frontline.
Reversal of Fortune shields an ally anywhere and flies you to them — use it to save a diving carry or to teleport into a fight.
Alacrity gives a shield and attack speed — throw it on your carry as they commit so the AS window matches their damage.
Consecrated Ground shields everyone inside — drop it where your team will actually fight, like an objective or chokepoint.
Manage your stance with Zero Shift — ranged to poke and kite safely, melee to burst when you can secure a kill, then shift back out.
Build Heat with Helios Core before objectives — hitting 300 unlocks Limit Break, so you want fights to start when it's charged.
Don't overstay in melee — swap in to delete a target, then Zero Shift back to ranged safety before their team answers.
Stack Souls early with Siphon on minions and camps — each soul permanently grows your health and damage, so farm it relentlessly.
Subjugate's root is your setup — land it, then Colossal Blow to displace, or follow with your team's burst.
Phantom Rush is engage and escape — dash in to start with Subjugate, but keep it for the exit if the fight goes south.
Weave abilities to refresh Rocket Dash — every bit of damage drops its cooldown, so you stay slippery if you keep hitting things.
Gunslinger fires your next basic three times after a cast — always cast, then auto, especially behind a Vaporize.
Ventilate locks you into a barrage — pop it when you're safe to free-fire, ideally after the enemy's engage is spent.
Your turret is your zone — place Flame Turret in a bush or behind terrain so enemies must walk through your fire to kill it.
Combo Oil Spill with Molotov or Inferno — igniting the oil multiplies your DoT; don't waste the oil on its own.
You can't escape (Mobility 1) — stack Badlands Critter slick to slow divers, and never push past your turret's protection.
Static Trap is your safety net — drop it at your flank or escape route so divers root themselves trying to reach you (Mobility 2).
Long Arm of the Law has unlimited range — snipe low targets across the map or secure objectives from safety.
Hot Pursuit is both kite and chase — pop it to ramp attack speed in a fight, not only to run.
Shadow Slip's recast is your escape — engage with the first cast but save the second to dash out, or you'll dive in with no way back (Durability 2).
Feast suppresses for 0.75s and scales with the target's health — leap it onto an isolated carry, not into the middle of their team.
Blade Siphon heals per enemy hit — fight in minion waves or clustered enemies to sustain through trades.
Always set up Curse with Mark — the ult only pays off on a marked target, so apply Mark in the fight before you commit it.
Swarm is your escape and engage — the speed and damage reduction let you dive in, drop your combo, and slip out.
Pestilence is percent-max-health DoT — it shreds tanks, so your damage stays relevant even into beefy comps.
Unstoppable Force cleanses CC and makes you unstoppable — save it to break a lockdown and turn the fight, not to engage.
Wild Rush charges up — hold it to threaten a long dash, but a full channel telegraphs hard, so mix in short ones.
Build Hersir stacks to empower your basic — weave autos between abilities for the bonus damage and shield.
Wait for Death Mark to detonate — your burst lands hardest when the mark's bonus damage triggers, so don't dump everything instantly.
Shadow Walk is engage and escape — approach unseen, but keep a Shadow Dance charge to backflip out (Durability 1, you die if caught).
Pick isolated targets — you delete a single squishy but have no team-fight presence, so avoid diving grouped enemies.
You're a zone-control mage — lay Slow Grenades and Land Mine on chokes and objectives to control space before the fight starts.
Make It Rain lets you move while raining missiles — kite backward during the channel so you deal damage without getting caught.
Weave Armed Salvo's empowered basic between spells for extra burst and to proc item effects.
Stack Vengeance with basics and abilities, then cash it out — your damage ramps per stack, so commit your rotation on one target.
Shadow Glide makes you briefly unselectable — time it to dodge a key skillshot or ult, not just to reposition.
Mercy benefits from lifesteal and crits — it's your sustain and burst in long fights; weave it for healing when you're low.
You out-sustain extended fights — Invigorate cleanses CC and heals, so bait the enemy's burst, cleanse, and keep swinging.
Cull's channel stuns and hits six times — line it up on clustered enemies, and use it when you won't get peeled off.
Long fights favor you — dive auto-attackers and tanks you can grind down, not burst mages who delete you before your sustain matters.
Seismic Assault's knock-up is your peel and setup — land it to start a combo or to stop a diver (Mobility 1, you have no escape).
Neural Disruptor deals more to low-health targets and silences — use it to execute and to shut down a key cast.
Void Drone drains enemy mana while Void Bomb restores yours — use them to win the sustained mana war against other mages.
Bull Rush into Shield Slam is your engage chain — knock a target up, then slam to lock the area down for your team.
Force Shield blocks all enemy projectiles — drop it to nullify an enemy ADC or skillshot mage and protect your backline.
Cybernetic Shell refreshes when you avoid damage — peek and trade, then break vision to reset the shield and re-engage.
Black Hole is a setup ult, not a snipe — drop it after a teammate's lockdown or your own Void Breach slow so they can't walk out.
Torn Space is escape and engage — keep it to reposition when divers reach you (Durability 1), and remember allies can follow you through.
Land an ability, then use the empowered basic (Cosmic Power) to tether and slow a fleeing target back into your combo.
You're a pick support — land Stasis Bomb's stun to start the play, then Containment Fence to trap them in or wall off their escape.
Containment Fence cuts both ways: block a diver chasing your carry, or fence the enemy in so your team can collapse.
Cast abilities to refresh Rocket Boots faster — weaving spells gives you frequent jumps to dodge and reposition.
Knock, Knock! pierces terrain and is your main damage — fish for it from fog or around walls where they cannot react, and it lifesteals to keep you healthy.
Surprise, Surprise! camouflages you and resets on takedowns — use it to set up a new Knock, Knock! angle or to escape, and chain it across kills.
You are extremely squishy (Durability 1) — Back It Up! banishes a target from afar; play at max range and poke with your sniper, never brawl up close.
Barricade gives 75% damage reduction — pop it to tank the enemy's burst as you engage, then recast to swing for damage.
Coliseum traps a target and doubles your Gladiator stacks — ult an isolated enemy so their team can't help while you ramp.
Spear of Nyr deals bonus damage to low-HP targets — use it to finish fleeing enemies from range.
You have no escape (Mobility 1) — land Dread Nova's stun or Ether Crystal's push to peel before anyone reaches you, and sit at the very back of fights.
Synaptic Obliterator roots you in a long channel but makes you push/pull immune — fire it from behind your frontline, never out in the open.
Chain abilities on one target to ramp Disintegrate — your damage grows the longer you keep hitting them, so commit a full rotation.
Neural Regenesis can revive an ally from lethal damage — pre-cast it on your carry before the big hit, not after they're dead.
Ricochet bounces between targets and stuns — aim it where it can chain to multiple enemies for a multi-stun.
Infuse heals more on low-health allies — hold it for when your carry is actually low rather than topping them off early.
Get in close — Crushing Maw deals 1.5x damage and shreds armor point-blank, so commit to the dive instead of poking from range.
Consume locks you in a self-slowing channel — only ult after Web Lash's CC lands or with an ally peeling, or you'll get bursted mid-channel.
Stack Pact of the Devourer's Hunger on minions/jungle before a fight — the ability haste lets you chain your full combo once you commit.
Auto-attack between every ability — Cross Crunch makes your next basic shave your ability cooldowns, so weaving autos is how you fit a full combo into one fight.
Engage with Forward Crunch for the stun, then Left Crunch → auto → Right Crunch; line it up so Right Crunch lands empowered for the uppercut knock-up.
Re-Crunch heals you on every 3rd hit, so you win long brawls — but you are weak early, so farm and dodge 50/50 fights until your ult and items come online.
Save Forward Crunch as your escape when diving the backline — burning it only to engage leaves you stuck with no way out.
Your Link is everything — keep it on your carry to share power and healing, and use the reactivate-pull to yank to safety or to them.
Hyperflux turns your carry into a monster — pop it on them as a fight starts; it's effectively a second ult for your ADC.
Psychic Flare blinds enemies facing you — use it to negate an auto-attacker's damage during a dive.
Stone Forged Soul heals more the lower you are — bait the enemy into committing, drop low, then stasis-heal and turn the fight.
Make Way shreds armor over its duration — drop it under a target you're diving so your team's follow-up hits harder.
Stoicism deflects a basic attack on a timer — trade into auto-attackers when it's up to win extended duels.
Swap stances for the moment — Spirit for mobility and poke, Body for durability and hard engage; you're strongest fluidly switching mid-fight.
Cyclebreaker / High Impact gives damage reduction — use it to tank the enemy's burst as you dive.
Your clones (84000 Hairs) add chaos and soak focus — ult in a teamfight where the extra bodies create confusion.
Berserk rewards fighting low — you spike hard below 40% HP, so don't disengage the moment you're chunked; many duels flip in your favor.
Kingslayer locks on when it hits an enemy hero — use it to catch fleeing targets, but you self-slow on the wind-up, so commit when they can't dodge.
Lead with Skull Crusher to gap-close, then weave basic attacks for the attack-speed steroid.
Open with Smash 'n Grab to pull enemies in, then Crush for the knock-up — that chain locks a target down long enough to commit.
Warlord's Challenge stuns then gives huge attack speed — ult to start the fight, then auto-attack to stack Bloodlust bleeds.
Stack Bloodlust on the enemy frontline early — the bleed is chip damage that sustains you and softens tanks.
Build Track stacks with abilities before ulting — All-Kill's wolves hit harder the more Track you've applied, so poke first, then commit.
Circle Rhythm shields you while dealing damage — use it as you dive so you survive the burst on the way in.
Rushing Beat has two charges — keep one to escape after you commit, rather than burning both on the engage.
Apply Scar first — your Obliterate missiles prioritize marked targets, so mark, then fire for focused burst.
Reckoning is a 1v1 ult — pull an isolated target into the Nether Realm where their team can't help; never use it in their group.
You're completely immobile (Mobility 1) — position behind your frontline; if you're caught with Scar/Reckoning down, you're dead.
Your power is the blade in the world — Judgement of the Heavens tethers a target to it, so land that, then Surge in to follow up.
Surge of the Heavens makes you unselectable and recalls the blade — use it to dodge burst and reposition, then re-throw.
Stack Strike of the Heavens by hitting enemies with abilities — the energized basics add burst, so weave autos between casts.
Rad Rounds is your damage window — activate it before committing so your basics ramp the irradiate stacks; weave it between abilities.
Gag Grenade's silence shuts down dashes and combos — save it to stop an assassin diving you, not as poke.
You're glass (Durability 1) — Old Rusty's slow is your peel; kite back with it the instant someone closes in.
Detection Protocol's scent trail lets you close from off-screen — use it to set up ganks, but it's a 50s cooldown, so pick the right target.
Pop Primeval Roar as you dive — the damage reduction tanks their burst, and the barrier explosion adds to your follow-up.
Ursine Burst is a point-and-click pick — grab a squishy and displace them onto your team; don't waste it on the tank.
Apply Soul Rip with abilities, then Severance into a marked target to reset its cooldown — chaining resets is how you stick to kills.
Cast an ability, then basic-attack — Hallowed Strikes gives you fast ranged autos, so weave them for damage and safe poke.
Soulstorm pulls enemies together — use it to group a clustered team for your AoE or to drag a fleeing target back.
Ascend, then dive, drops you onto a backliner from range — use it to gap-close onto a target who thinks they're safe.
Heresy ramps your damage over its duration — pop it as you commit, and Fallen Grace refunds it on a takedown, so chain kills to stay in form.
You're Durability 1 — get in, delete a squishy, and use your mobility to leave; don't try to brawl the frontline.
Lash Kick pulls you to a target — gap-close onto a backliner, or whip to the world to escape over terrain.
Windburn's every-third-hit deals percent-max-health and heals you — keep attacking the same target to shred tanks and sustain.
Quelling Gale blocks enemy projectiles — pop it to negate an enemy ADC or skillshot during your dive.
Stack Relentless by attacking one target — your damage ramps the longer you focus the same enemy, so lock on in fights.
Heightened Senses is your fight button — pop it for the attack-speed spike and bonus every-second-hit damage before committing.
You have zero mobility (Mobility 1) — Hail of Arrows zones divers off you; your only safety is never stepping ahead of your frontline.
You're immobile (Mobility 1) — Energy Shield is your lifeline; hold it to block the enemy's engage (a hook or stun), not for poke.
Swap modes for the situation — Sentry to ramp attack speed safely, Assault for burst and the displacement shot.
GTFO self-slows during the channel — fire it from max range at a low target, never mid-fight where you'll be punished.
Safeguard is a spellshield — time it to eat the enemy's key ability (a stun or burst), which also doubles your stat gain.
Earth Shatter executes below its HP threshold — track the number and ult to secure kills on targets trying to flee low.
Reaping Dash scales with the target's current health — open with it on a healthy enemy, then Hamstring to stick to them.
Abduct is a long, telegraphed pull — channel it from fog or behind terrain, then drag a key target back to your team.
Contagion heals you and allies over time — pre-cast it before engaging so the sustain is already ticking in the fight.
Eclipse blinds the enemy team's vision — use it to set up a flank or disengage, leaning on the move speed since you're slow (Mobility 1).
Maco Gift hurts enemies or heals allies depending on who it hits — aim it at your carry in trades, or poke with it when allies are healthy.
Build Mach Speed by staying on the move — at max stacks you get slow immunity, making you a slippery peeler.
You're mobile, not tanky — use your speed to body-block and reposition allies, not to frontline.
Open fights with Cryoseism — the slow-then-stun gives a long lockdown window; chain Hoarfrost's root after it to keep them pinned.
Glacial Charge is your engage and your team's highway — the ice trail speeds up allies, so use it to start dives, not just to escape.
Use Frozen Simulacrum's mid-air jump to dodge skillshots; its cooldown drops when you're immobilized, so it's a reliable reset.
Hypercharge converts attack speed into fixed-rate on-hit damage — pop it for steady DPS in long fights, especially against tanks.
Air Assault makes you hover and fire missiles — use the height to dodge ground CC while still dealing damage.
You're Durability 1 — keep your jumps and Air Assault for dodging engage; your survival is positioning, not stats.
Keep Song Of My People toggled in lane and fights — the team heal scales with Rhythm stacks, so build them by hitting things.
March's speed is your engage and disengage — use it to catch enemies for a Thunk stun or to peel your carry out.
Crash Bang Boom is a teamfight ult — channel it inside a grouped enemy, but you're vulnerable during it, so have peel.
